dreams: A passionate love
lessons_attracted: A lack pattern with someone who comes in and out of her life; learning to let go, stay present, and practice pure love and appreciation
life_events: Career, friends, family, finances, and glow-up feel strong; intense spiritual journey over the last two months
notes: Venus Virgo 4H as stated, not whole-sign consistent
text: Locked in as soon as you said going through it. Taurus rising, 4th house Venus but in Virgo, 4th house nn in Leo, 1st house Taurus Saturn. I have an amazing career, friends, family, finance, even an incredible glow up, but a passionate love is alluding me. Met someone who is like a stone thrown on water coming in and out of my life who feels perfect but what I’m attracting is lack with that right now. I’ve been on a ramped up spiritual journey the last two months as a result where my entire worldviews related to manifestation, the way I’ve seen the world, and how to get my desires, or even what my desires are, has shifted, multiple times, sometimes daily. The lesson I am feeling right now is that I need to let go. Past, future, forget it. Presence is what I’m trying to practice. Pure love and appreciation for the present moment.

dreams: To pursue her passions without worrying about financial upkeep, keep learning across cultures and mediums, have adventure and novelty, and return to slow comfort with someone grounding
lessons_attracted: Loss, not taking things so seriously, giving less than she receives, enjoying the present, and looking forward to what comes next
life_events: Lost her grandmother young, lost all family pets within days during the pandemic, had friendship losses, has never had a romantic relationship by 25, and moved to a different country with a different language
notes: Also mentions Taurus Sun, Virgo Moon, Sagittarius 8H stellium, and Capricorn Moon 9H
text: taurus rising, venus and saturn in taurus in the first house, as well. north node and sun in cancer in the third house. I also have a stellium in sagittarius (mars, pluto, chiron) in the eighth house, and moon in capricorn in the ninth house. Lost my grandmother who was part of my everyday life at a young age which made me question a lot, and then lost all of my family pets within a few days of eachother during the pandemic. I’ve had various friendship losses (though a few by my own fault). I’ve never been in a romantic relationship and I have just turned 25. I tend to be a very lucky person, I always find out how to make things work but, I have a tendency to think I’m not doing enough or that I’m behind. In the past year, I have moved to a different country with a different language and I have felt a shift of genuine happiness and sense of self that I had not felt in a long time. I don’t think I’ve had a hard life and I’m very grateful for my experiences and privileges that I have. I want to find and pursue what I’m passionate about and not have to worry about the financial upkeep and I want to keep learning about all sorts of things whether that’s through schooling or through other mediums across cultures, language, food, and the world. I want the chance of adventure and new things but also the ability to fall back to my safe space of slow comfort with someone who grounds me and can match my shifting energy. In the past, I believe that I have attracted lessons of loss, learning to not take things so seriously, and stop giving more than I receive, and to enjoy the present for what it is and look forward for what is next to come. :’)

text:
Venus in Aries 11th house, north node cancer 2nd house, Saturn in Taurus 1st house as a Taurus Rising
My dream hmm! I want to impact people, I want to help people. I want to make so much money that it serves to impact people's life. I would also want to set my table and have a community ( just working towards my fear of being seen and my shyness).
To be honest, I hate the conventional way of work.
I have attracted more wounded people (but finally learnt the hard way that it is not my job to save them). Also I used to easily get attached but I am learning to be detached when someone comes in my life and let it flow. I'm still an emotional person but I have to accept the fact that people might not be here to stay.
